Motion For Protective Order Staying The Deposition Of Pltf And Preventing Pltfs From Questioning Deft Using Evidence Obtained Unlawfully, For Order To Disqualifying Pltf'S Counsel For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-13-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant's Motion For Protective Order Staying The Deposition Of Plaintiff and Preventing Plaintiffs From Questioning Defendant Using Evidence Obtained Unlawfully, and For An Order Disqualifying Plaintiff's Counsel: is granted in part and denied in part. As the motion seeks an order disqualifying counsel, such an order must be sought from the Law and Motion Department. As the motion seeks to bar the use of any evidence obtained by the entry into the premises, the motion is granted. All requests for monetary sanctions are denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
